summary: Small repository/build-system cleanup PR. It stopped generating a preprocessed linker script under the build directory and instead used the root `ldscript.lcf` directly, removing the Makefile `CPP` helper and the rule that transformed `ldscript.lcf` into `$(BUILD_DIR)/ldscript.lcf`. It also narrowed the ignored MWCC compiler path from `mwcc_compiler/` to `/tools/mwcc_compiler`. No PR body or review-comment text was available in the slice, so rationale beyond the diff is limited.
what_changed: Changed `.gitignore` so the ignored compiler directory is `/tools/mwcc_compiler` instead of a top-level or anywhere-matching `mwcc_compiler/` entry.
Changed `LDSCRIPT` in `Makefile` from `$(BUILD_DIR)/ldscript.lcf` to `ldscript.lcf`, making the build consume the source linker script directly.
Removed platform-specific `CPP := $(DEVKITPPC)/bin/powerpc-eabi-cpp... -P` assignments for Windows and non-Windows builds.
Removed the Makefile rule that generated `$(LDSCRIPT)` from `ldscript.lcf` using `CPP -MMD -MP -MT ... -DBUILD_DIR=$(BUILD_DIR)`.
Net change was very small: 2 files, 2 added lines, 7 deleted lines.
smart_moves: Simplified the build graph by eliminating an apparently unnecessary generated linker-script artifact in `$(BUILD_DIR)`.
Reduced platform-specific Makefile surface area by removing `CPP` setup that was only needed for the deleted linker-script preprocessing rule.
Made the compiler-binary ignore rule more precise by anchoring it to the intended `tools` location, reducing the chance of accidentally ignoring unrelated `mwcc_compiler` paths elsewhere in the tree.
decomp_lessons: Prefer direct source inputs in the build when preprocessing or generated intermediates are not necessary; fewer generated files means fewer dependency edges and less platform-specific tool configuration.
Keep `.gitignore` rules as specific as practical for checked-in tooling layouts, especially when tool names could plausibly appear in source or documentation paths.
Removing a generated artifact may also allow removal of its dependency-generation machinery, compiler/preprocessor variables, and cross-platform conditionals.
naming_conventions: `BUILD_DIR`, `VANILLA_DIR`, `PROFILE_DIR`, `LDSCRIPT`, `DOL`, `MWCC_LD_VERSION`, `WINE`, `DEVKITPPC`, and `SHA1SUM` are Makefile-style uppercase variable names used for build configuration.
The linker script is named `ldscript.lcf` and, after this PR, is referenced directly as `LDSCRIPT := ldscript.lcf`.
The ignored MWCC compiler directory is expected at `/tools/mwcc_compiler`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `ldscript.lcf` to see whether direct use of the linker script remained the long-term build convention.
Search for later changes to `/tools/mwcc_compiler` handling or documentation to understand the expected local compiler install layout.
Check surrounding Makefile history for why linker-script preprocessing was introduced and why it became unnecessary by PR 551.
